holes.content:
On DeepScaleR-1.5B with CIB using a larger 7B prior model, average accuracy decreases from 44.7% (baseline) to 44.0% (−0.7pp), while average token count decreases from 6,722 to 3,951 tokens (−41%). This demonstrates aggressive compression with minimal accuracy cost:

| Benchmark | Acc (base) | Acc (CIB-7B) | Tok (base) | Tok (CIB-7B) |
|-----------|-----------|-------------|-----------|-------------|
| MATH500   | 85.8%     | 84.0%       | 3,190     | 1,548       |
| AIME24    | 38.1%     | 35.2%       | 9,269     | 5,720       |
| AIME25    | 25.2%     | 25.8%       | 8,901     | 5,362       |
| Minerva   | 19.9%     | 19.6%       | 6,375     | 3,097       |
| Olympiad  | 54.7%     | 55.2%       | 5,875     | 4,029       |
holes.label: deepscaler_7b_prior

holes.content:
On DeepScaleR-1.5B with CIB using 1.5B prior ($\beta^+ = 1.5\times10^{-4}$, aggressive), average accuracy improves from 44.7% (baseline) to 46.0% (+1.3pp), while average token count decreases from 6,722 (baseline) to 4,797 tokens (−29%):

| Benchmark | Acc (base) | Acc (CIB) | Tok (base) | Tok (CIB) |
|-----------|-----------|-----------|-----------|----------|
| MATH500   | 85.8%     | 87.8%     | 3,190     | 2,106    |
| AIME24    | 38.1%     | 40.2%     | 9,269     | 6,857    |
| AIME25    | 25.2%     | 26.3%     | 8,901     | 6,672    |
| Minerva   | 19.9%     | 20.3%     | 6,375     | 4,144    |
| Olympiad  | 54.7%     | 55.3%     | 5,875     | 4,208    |
holes.label: deepscaler_cib_aggressive

holes.content: CIB with semantic prior predicts higher accuracy than L1-Exact at comparable compression levels because semantic cost selectively removes low-information tokens while preserving task-critical reasoning steps.
holes.label: pred_cib_better

holes.content: L1-Exact predicts equal or greater compression at the cost of higher accuracy loss than CIB, because it cannot distinguish essential from redundant tokens.
holes.label: pred_l1_worse

holes.content: Transformer decoder architectures use causal self-attention that allows the answer generation step to attend directly to both the input prompt X and the reasoning trace Z via the key-value cache. This means the Markov chain assumption Y↔X↔Z required by standard IB is violated: Y depends on (X,Z) jointly rather than on Z alone.
holes.label: transformer_attention_violates_markov
